artifact: IEC 62304 Traceability Matrix (variant A: evidence array per requirement)

rows.requirement_text: Forward collision warning vs. a lead vehicle must operate at any forward speed greater than 10 km/h (6.2 mph) and less than 145 km/h (90.1 mph) (§ 571.127 S5.1.1).

evidence.caveat: Formally proven against the stated specification.
evidence.proof_content_caveat: Definitional proof: the postcondition restates the implementation (ensures is equivalent to the body), so the proof obligation is a tautology discharged by reflexivity. Certified: totality, type-safety, match-exhaustiveness, and the literal boundary structure of the definition. NOT certified: an independent property beyond the definition, or fidelity to the source requirement.

rows.requirement_text: Automatic emergency braking vs. a lead vehicle must operate at any forward speed greater than 10 km/h (6.2 mph) and less than 145 km/h (90.1 mph) (§ 571.127 S5.1.2) - a distinct obligation from REQ-AEB-1 (the AEB action, not the FCW warning), confirmed against the source text even though the numeric envelope coincides.

rows.requirement_text: Subject vehicle braking onset is the point at which the subject vehicle achieves a deceleration of 0.15 g due to automatic control of the service brakes (§ 571.127 S4).

rows.requirement_text: False activation: the vehicle must not automatically apply braking that results in peak additional deceleration exceeding what manual braking would produce by 0.25 g or greater (§ 571.127 S5.3).

rows.requirement_text: Vehicle-class eligibility: this standard applies to passenger cars and to multipurpose passenger vehicles, trucks, and buses with a GVWR of 4,536 kg (10,000 lb) or less (§ 571.127 S3). Named, sourced, not formalized as a Dafny signature - this is a vehicle-class gate on whether § 571.127 applies at all, not a claim the AEB kernel's own logic must satisfy.
rows.code_location: none - prose only, not yet formalized

evidence.scope_text: Vehicle-class eligibility (GVWR threshold) is a fleet/homologation fact checked once per vehicle model, not a runtime decision the AEB kernel evaluates per detection event - out of this kernel's proof scope by construction, mirroring dosage_calculator's kernel_scope/system_scope split (REQ-GIP-1-4-12).
evidence.note: system_scope declared but not evidenced at this phase; deferred to integration testing (explicit GAP, not omission)

notes: no evidence bound for this requirement
system_scope declared but not evidenced at this phase; deferred to integration testing (explicit GAP, not omission)

rows.requirement_text: Malfunction detection and controls: the system must continuously detect malfunctions (including sensor-obstruction-caused performance degradation) and provide a telltale notification; the system must always return to its default AEB mode at the start of each ignition cycle except for two named exceptions (§ 571.127 S5.4). Named, sourced, not formalized as a Dafny signature.
rows.code_location: none - prose only, not yet formalized

evidence.scope_text: A stateful, continuous-monitoring/mode-control requirement (system state across time and ignition cycles), not a pure function of a single detection event's inputs - a genuinely different kind of claim than REQ-AEB-1..8, not a smaller version of the same kind. Would need a state-machine modeling approach if formalized, not the pure-function style this kernel currently uses throughout.
evidence.note: system_scope declared but not evidenced at this phase; deferred to integration testing (explicit GAP, not omission)
